transform.name: transform
transform.hover_info: Transformation applied to the element (position, rotation, scale).

position.name: position
position.hover_info: 3D position in world space. Format: "x y z"

rotation.name: rotation
rotation.hover_info: Rotation. Format depends on type attribute.

variants.hover_info:
Surface rotation, expressed as a unit quaternion (x y z w).

The quaternion must be normalized : x² + y² + z² + w² = 1.
Unnormalized values will be rejected by the parser.

link.name: link
link.hover_info: Link another scene file. It allow you to split scene content across multiple files.

path.name: path
path.hover_info: The path of the linked file.

materials.name: materials
materials.hover_info: List of materials in the scene. Contains <material> tags.

material.name: material
material.hover_info: Material definition with all its properties.

name.name: name
name.hover_info: Unique identifier for this material. Used to reference it in objects.

albedo.name: albedo
albedo.hover_info: Base surface color that defines the proportion of incoming light reflected diffusely. It does not include lighting, shadows, reflections, or emission — only the intrinsic reflectance of the material.

metallic.name: metallic
metallic.hover_info: Whether the surface behaves as a conductor (1.0) or a dielectric (0.0). Metals have no diffuse component and their reflectance is tinted by the albedo. Intermediate values are physically incorrect but accepted as an artistic control.

roughness.name: roughness
roughness.hover_info: Microscale irregularity of the surface. 0.0 is a perfect mirror, 1.0 is fully diffuse. Drives the width of the specular lobe via the GGX distribution.

transmission.name: transmission
transmission.hover_info: Fraction of light that passes through the surface rather than being reflected. 1.0 is fully transparent. Requires a valid IOR to refract correctly.

ambient_occlusion.name: ambient_occlusion
ambient_occlusion.hover_info: Multiplier that darkens crevices by approximating how much ambient light reaches a point. Not physically based — It allows you to add texture to a surface that doesn't really have any. Useful as a baked artistic control.

emission_strength.name: emission_strength
emission_strength.hover_info: Power of the light emitted by the surface. A value above 0 turns the surface into an area light that contributes to global illumination.

emission_color.name: emission_color
emission_color.hover_info: Color of the emitted light. Multiplied by emission_strength to give the final radiance.

ior.name: ior
ior.hover_info: Index of Refraction. Controls how much light bends when entering the surface and the fresnel reflectance at grazing angles. 1.0 is vacuum, common values are 1.33 (water), 1.5 (glass), 2.4 (diamond). Only meaningful for dielectrics.

texture_scale.name: texture_scale
texture_scale.hover_info: Uniform scale applied to UV coordinates before sampling any texture. Values above 1.0 tile the texture, below 1.0 zoom in.

normal_map.name: normal_map
normal_map.hover_info: Tangent-space normal map. RGB values are interpreted as XYZ perturbations of the surface normal, remapped from [0,1] to [-1,1].

normal_intensity.name: normal_intensity
normal_intensity.hover_info: Blending factor between the geometry normal (0.0) and the fully perturbed normal from the normal map (1.0). Behavior with values outside the range [0-1] is undefined.

assets.name: assets
assets.hover_info: List of assets in the scene.

asset.name: asset
asset.hover_info: Asset definition. Type determines available children.

type.name: type
type.hover_info: Type of asset: object (mesh from file), primitive (built-in shape), or instance (reference to another asset).

material.name: material
material.hover_info: Reference to the material of the asset

variants.hover_info: Object asset: loads a mesh from a file.

normal.name: normal
normal.hover_info: Define the orientation of the plane

variants.hover_info: Instance asset: reference to another asset with a different transform.

name.name: name
name.hover_info: Camera unique identifier, used to set the render camera

variants.hover_info: Easy camera placement defined with a lookat

target.name: target
target.hover_info: Camera target/look at. Represent a point of the world where the camera is looking.

up.name: up
up.hover_info: Camera up vector. Used to set a roll to the camera.

variants.hover_info: Camera placement defined with a rotation

rotation.name: rotation
rotation.hover_info: Rotation. Format depends on type attribute.

variants.hover_info:
Surface rotation, expressed as a unit quaternion (x y z w).

The quaternion must be normalized : x² + y² + z² + w² = 1.
Unnormalized values will be rejected by the parser.
A perspective camera and its properties.

fov.name: fov
fov.hover_info: Field of view of the camera

sensor_fit.name: sensor_fit
sensor_fit.hover_info: Direction of the sensor used. If the sensor fit is horizontal, the sensor height will be adapted to match the resolution of the render. And that's the opposite with a vertical sensor fit.

sensor_width.name: sensor_width
sensor_width.hover_info: Width of the sensor of your camera

sensor_height.name: sensor_height
sensor_height.hover_info: Height of the sensor of your camera

f_stop.name: f_stop
f_stop.hover_info:
Aperture of the lens, expressed as an f-stop (f/N).

Controls the radius of the sampling disk on the sensor —
the wider the aperture, the shallower the depth of field.

- f/1.4  wide open — strong bokeh, very shallow focus
- f/2.8
- f/5.6
- f/8    stopped down — sharp throughout, minimal bokeh
- f/16

shutter_speed.name: shutter_speed
shutter_speed.hover_info: Change the shutter speed (used for motion blur in animations)

variants.hover_info: An orthographic camera and its properties.

ortho_scale.name: ortho_scale
ortho_scale.hover_info: Width of the visible zone (in world units)

variants.hover_info: A fisheye camera and its properties.
A panoramic camera and its properties.

ref.name: ref
ref.hover_info: ID of the camera used for the render

samples.name: samples
samples.hover_info: Amount of samples for the render
samples.completion_detail: Amount of samples for the render

name.name: name
name.hover_info: Unique identifier for the texture. Used as a reference for materials using it

lights.name: lights
lights.hover_info:
Container for all analytical lights in the scene.
Emissive surfaces are defined at the material level and are not listed here.

light.name: light
light.hover_info: Defines an analytical light source. The expected children depend on the type attribute.

color.name: color
color.hover_info: Spectral color of the emitted light.

intensity.name: intensity
intensity.hover_info:
Power of the light source.

For point lights, intensity falls off with the inverse square of the distance.
For directional lights, intensity is uniform regardless of distance.

Multiplied by color to give the final emitted radiance.

variants.hover_info:
Omnidirectional light emitting equally in all directions from a single point.
Intensity falls off with the square of the distance (inverse square law).

position.name: position
position.hover_info: World-space position of the light source.

variants.hover_info:
Infinitely distant light source emitting parallel rays in a uniform direction.
Models large, far away sources such as the sun or moon.
Has no position — only direction and intensity matter.

direction.name: direction
direction.hover_info:
World-space direction the light is emitting toward, as a normalized vec3.
For example (0 -1 0) points straight down.

environment.name: environment
environment.hover_info:
Defines the background and ambient lighting of the scene.
Contributes to global illumination — rays that escape all geometry sample this instead.

variants.hover_info:
Spherical environment map used as the scene background and indirect light source.
The texture is projected onto an infinite sphere.
Expects a high dynamic range image (.exr, .hdr) for physically accurate lighting.

skybox.name: skybox
skybox.hover_info:
Spherical environment map used as the scene background and indirect light source.
The texture is projected onto an infinite sphere.
Expects a high dynamic range image (.exr, .hdr) for physically accurate lighting.

rotation.name: rotation
rotation.hover_info: Skybox rotation around the y axe

variants.hover_info:
Radiance of the background, in linear RGB.
Values above 1.0 are valid and act as an emissive background contributing to global illumination.

- 0 0 0      black background, no ambient contribution
- 1 1 1      white, uniformly lit scene
- 0.5 0.7 1  light blue sky approximation

color.name: color
color.hover_info:
Radiance of the background, in linear RGB.
Values above 1.0 are valid and act as an emissive background contributing to global illumination.

- 0 0 0      black background, no ambient contribution
- 1 1 1      white, uniformly lit scene
- 0.5 0.7 1  light blue sky approximation
